title: 9 Herbal Essential Oils For Motion Sickness
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great natural remedy for motion sickness. But how do they work? Some herbal teas, like those made from Ginkgo biloba, have been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, which can help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness. Ginkgo biloba also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce nausea and dizziness.  Another herb that's commonly used to relieve motion sickness is Zingiber officinale, or ginger. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to calm the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting. Many people also use ginger tea or ginger essential oil to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.  Valeriana officinalis, or valerian root, is another herb that can help to calm the body and mind, making it easier to deal with motion sickness. Valerian root has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to motion sickness.  Using herbal essential oils for motion sickness can be a convenient and natural way to manage symptoms. These oils can be applied topically, inhaled, or added to a warm bath for relaxation. They can also be used in combination with other natural remedies, like acupressure or meditation, to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for motion sickness

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils contains the bioactive constituents bilobalide, ginkgolides, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce nausea and vomiting. The flavonoids in ginkgo biloba essential oils have a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, reducing muscle spasms and cramping. Bilobalide also has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate dizziness and disorientation. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, ginkgo biloba essential oils can help to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil).
Combine the Ginkgo biloba leaves and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Make sure the leaves are fully covered by the oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the extracted oil to a small glass bottle and label it. Use 5-7 drops of the oil in a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for topical use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains the bioactive constituents gingerol and shogaol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. The oils also have a sedative effect, which can calm the stomach and reduce queasiness. Gingerol's ability to inhibit serotonin receptors can also help regulate digestive function and prevent nausea. The properties of Zingiber officinale essential oils make it a potential natural remedy for motion sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh ginger roots and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Wash the ginger roots and chop them into small pieces. Use 1 tablespoon of chopped ginger for every 1 cup of carrier oil.
Combine the chopped ginger and carrier oil in a saucepan and heat it over low heat for 2-3 hours, or until the oil turns golden brown.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, squeezing the cheesecloth to get all the oil.
Store the Zingiber officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, and use 5-10 drops as needed to alleviate motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils contains valerenic acid, valeranone, and isovaleric acid as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have sedative and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness. The sedative properties can calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, while the antispasmodic properties can relax the muscles and reduce nausea. The calming effect of valerenic acid can also help regulate the body's balance and equilibrium, making it easier to manage the physical effects of motion sickness. By reducing anxiety and nausea, Valeriana officinalis essential oils may help to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness and promote a sense of calm.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Valeriana officinalis roots, dried and crushed.
Combine the crushed roots with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as jojoba oil or sweet almond oil, in a clean glass container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the Valeriana officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and use 5-7 drops in a diffuser or apply topically as needed to help with motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains a number of bioactive constituents that may help alleviate motion sickness symptoms. These constituents include linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and calming properties. The sedative effect of linalool and linalyl acetate can help reduce the anxiety and nausea associated with motion sickness. Additionally, the calming properties of these constituents may help slow down the body's response to motion, making it easier to tolerate. By reducing anxiety and nausea, and slowing down the body's response to motion, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may provide relief from motion sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 cups of water in a large pot.
Heat the water in the pot over low heat, then add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers and let it steep for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a glass jar with a lid and add 1 tablespoon of vodka or glycerin as a preservative.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the oil separate from the water.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains a compound called anethole, which is responsible for its calming properties. Anethole and another compound called fenchone help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues, often associated with motion sickness. Fenchone also has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to regulate the body's response to motion. The essential oil's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties can also help to calm the stomach and prevent nausea. By reducing inflammation and regulating the nervous system, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds (also known as fennel seeds) and 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil)
Place the fennel seeds in a clean glass jar with a lid and cover them with 2 cups of carrier oil
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar
Discard the fennel seeds and store the Foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains a compound called borneol, which has antispasmodic properties that help to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce nausea. The oils also contain camphor and linalool, which have a sedative effect and can help to calm the nervous system, reducing the symptoms of motion sickness. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of borneol and other constituents, such as pinocamphone, can also help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea. The sedative effect of linalool and other constituents can help to calm the body and mind, reducing the anxiety and stress that often accompany motion sickness. By reducing the symptoms of nausea and inflammation, and promoting relaxation, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils can provide relief from motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Wash the leaves with water and gently pat them dry with a paper towel.
Combine the leaves and carrier oil in a clean glass jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the oil infuse.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discard the solids and store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains limonene, beta-pinene, and beta-myrcene as its major bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness. Limonene, in particular, has been shown to have a calming effect on the body, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Beta-pinene and beta-myrcene also have a sedative effect, which can help calm the stomach and reduce dizziness. By using Cymbopogon citratus essential oils, individuals may experience relief from motion sickness symptoms due to its ability to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves, a clean glass jar, and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Wash the leaves with water and let them dry completely.
Pack the dried leaves into the glass jar, leaving 1 inch at the top.
Pour 2 cups of carrier oil over the leaves and close the jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, then strain and discard the leaves.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and lignans as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have a sedative and anti-inflammatory effect, which can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness. The flavonoids in Passiflora incarnata essential oils, such as vitexin and isovitexin, have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress. The alkaloids, including passiflorine and harmine, have a sedative effect, which can help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. By reducing anxiety, stress, and nausea, Passiflora incarnata essential oils can be used to help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal essential oils for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers. Clean and dry them.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil) to a clean glass jar.
Pack the dried Passiflora incarnata into a clean glass jar, making sure they are completely covered by the carrier oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and use the resulting oil for motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils contains the bioactive constituents quinine and quinidine. Quinine has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce nausea. Quinidine, on the other hand, has a sedative effect, which can help calm the mind and body, reducing the symptoms of motion sickness. The essential oil also contains alkaloids that can help regulate the digestive system and reduce the frequency of vomiting. By using Cinchona officinalis essential oils, you can help alleviate the discomfort and distress associated with motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal essential oils for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Cinchona officinalis bark, a glass jar with a lid, a cheesecloth, and a cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Dry the Cinchona bark in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, then crush it into small pieces.
Combine 1 cup of the crushed bark with 1 cup of carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. Strain the mixture through the cheesecloth into a clean container.
Discard the solids and use the liquid as your Cinchona officinalis essential oil for motion sickness. Store it in a dark glass bottle.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea might help prevent motion sickness. Peppermint tea, for example, has been shown to ease nausea. Ginger tea is also known to calm stomach problems. These teas may help your body adjust to changes in motion and reduce discomfort. Some people find they make a difference when traveling by car, boat, or plane.
faq_safe: While herbal teas like ginger and peppermint can help with motion sickness, it's not recommended to consume them daily for this purpose. These teas contain active ingredients that may interact with other medications or worsen conditions like high blood pressure or certain digestive issues. Moderation is key when using herbal remedies.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like ginger and peppermint can help with motion sickness. It's said that drinking a cup of these teas about 30 minutes before traveling might help. The effects might be felt within 15 to 30 minutes after consumption, but it's different for everyone. Some people might feel better sooner, while others might take a bit longer to notice any relief.
faq_time: If you're prone to motion sickness, try drinking herbal tea before you start traveling. Peppermint tea is a good choice because it can help calm your stomach. Drinking it about 30 minutes to an hour before you leave can be helpful. This can give the tea time to take effect and reduce your symptoms.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for motion sickness. For example, ginger tea may boost the effects of anti-nausea pills, making them stronger. On the other hand, teas like peppermint or chamomile might reduce the need for medication, but this can also be a problem if you need a strong dose.
faq_pregnant: Herbal teas for motion sickness can be a good alternative to medication, but it's essential to be cautious when pregnant. Peppermint and ginger teas are often used to calm nausea, but some herbal teas may interact with pregnancy hormones or stimulate the uterus. Choose teas that are specifically labeled as safe for pregnant women and consume them in moderation.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a natural way to ease motion sickness, but when breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, are often considered safe, but others may pass into breast milk and affect the baby. It's essential to research and understand the potential risks before consuming herbal teas for motion sickness.
title_2: 9 Essential Oils to Help Travelers Overcome Motion Sickness
